title: Ensure /etc/shadow password fields are not empty

prose.en: Ensure /etc/shadow password fields are not empty

description.en: An account with an empty password field means that anybody may log in as that user without providing a password.

rationale.en: All accounts must have passwords or be locked to prevent the account from being used by an unauthorized user.

audit.en:
If any accounts in the `/etc/shadow` file do not have a password, run the following command to lock the account until it can be determined why it does not have a password:  

Also, check to see if the account is logged in and investigate what it is being used for to determine if it needs to be forced off.

```
# passwd -l
```

remediation.en:
If any accounts in the `/etc/shadow` file do not have a password, run the following command to lock the account until it can be determined why it does not have a password:  

Also, check to see if the account is logged in and investigate what it is being used for to determine if it needs to be forced off.

```
# passwd -l
```
